geopolitics_summary: US extends Russian oil sanctions waiver amid Strait of Hormuz tensions; EU-US trade negotiations ongoing; energy policy fragmentation risks from far-right opposition to renewables permitting.

themes.headline: US extends Russian oil sanctions waiver through June amid Hormuz tensions
themes.why_it_matters: Continued waiver suppresses Brent crude; lower crude → lower gas-to-coal switching, reduced TTF support. Strait closure risk keeps oil premium live, affecting LNG netback arbitrage to NW Europe.

themes.headline: Italy seeks energy carveout from EU fiscal rules framework
themes.why_it_matters: If granted, could unlock state support for gas storage, LNG import terminals, or renewable buildout. Fiscal flexibility = higher capex for energy infrastructure, reducing supply-side risk on Cal+1 power.

themes.headline: Far-right coalition slows EU renewable permitting via environmental opposition
themes.why_it_matters: Political delays to wind/solar buildout tighten European generation capacity, supporting long-term power prices and widening thermal dispatch margins. DA spreads and Cal+1 curves vulnerable if permitting grinds to halt.

themes.headline: US industrial natural gas demand hits record highs through 2027
themes.why_it_matters: Rising US industrial gas consumption reduces LNG export availability to Europe; tightens global LNG supply. Higher US demand → lower netbacks to NW Europe, reducing LNG arbitrage incentive; TTF upside capped.

themes.headline: EU–US trade deal negotiations advance amid auto tariff deadline pressure
themes.why_it_matters: Resolution of auto tariff threat stabilizes macro risk backdrop for European power and gas; reduces currency/trade-war volatility. Indirect: lower macro uncertainty supports demand-sensitive gas bull markets (heating season prep).

watchlist: US Russian oil sanctions waiver renewal decision (June deadline); Strait of Hormuz escalation risk.
EU–Italy fiscal rule carveout negotiation outcome; energy capex impact.
EU renewable permitting reform progress; political delays to wind/solar capacity.
US LNG export volumes; industrial demand elasticity in H2 2026.
